item.title: Hong Kong Dollar Hits 10-Month Low as Fed View Buoys Greenback
item.description: The Hong Kong dollar fell to its weakest level against the US dollar in about 10 months, as a stronger greenback and expectations for further Federal Reserve rate hikes pressured Asian currencies.

item.title: Korean Stocks Tumble 8% as Chipmakers Slide, Trading Halted
item.description: South Korean stocks fell as renewed selling in chipmakers highlighted their heightened sensitivity to swings in global artificial-intelligence sentiment.

item.title: Iron Ore Set for Seventh Straight Weekly Loss as Market Softens
item.description: Iron ore headed for a seventh weekly loss — the worst run since 2022 — as demand weakened seasonally and mill margins narrowed.

item.title: Sherritt Warns of Going-Concern Risk After Trump Cuba Sanctions
item.description: A Canadian miner that is one of Cuba’s biggest foreign investors warned investors that its ability to carry on as a going concern is in doubt after Donald Trump expanded US sanctions on the Caribbean nation.

item.title: Half of Australian Adults Have Never Sought Retirement Advice
item.description: Australians are sleepwalking into their retirement, according to new research.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Vows to Keep Fiscal Discipline With Investment
item.description: The Japanese government will continue to maintain fiscal sustainability as it compiles budgets and makes investment decisions, the finance minister said, after Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi unveiled a $2.3 trillion, long-term investment roadmap along with sweeping budget reforms.

item.title: Chinese Energy Firm Pushes Fresh Debt Revamp After Payment Delay
item.description: Huachen Energy Co. plans to present a proposal to restructure its bonds, including $627 million in dollar-denominated notes, to investors as early as August, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Chinese power producer makes a fresh bid to ease liquidity pressure.

item.title: Samsung, SK Hynix Reportedly Preparing Huge AI Spending Push
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. are preparing to announce hundreds of billions of dollars worth in new investments on Monday, according to South Korean media reports this week.

item.title: SoftBank’s Shares Tumble After Report of OpenAI’s IPO Delay
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s stock fell as much as 13% on concerns that OpenAI may hold off on an initial public offering until next year and delay returns for its Japanese backer.

item.title: Weak Monsoon Emerges as India’s Next Economic Worry, After Oil
item.description: A weak monsoon is emerging as India’s next inflation risk, with a developing El Niño threatening to curb rainfall and push up food prices just as an easing oil relieves broader price pressures.

item.title: Cathay Bank Chairman Quits After Governance Dispute, Family Feud
item.description: Cathay United Bank Co. Chairman Kuo Ming-jian has resigned following a regulatory breach at the lender’s asset management affiliate linked to his outside positions, an incident that culminated in a physical altercation with a member of Taiwan’s billionaire Tsai family.

item.title: Gold Steadies Near $4,000 as Inflation Data Eases Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: Gold steadied near $4,000 an ounce as the latest US inflation gauge tempered expectations for an interest-rate hike, capping a tumultuous week that saw bullion slump to the lowest since November.

item.title: Yen Outperforms G-10 Peers Despite Hovering Near 1986 Low
item.description: The yen’s pause just shy of a four-decade low has yielded an unexpected result: it is the best-performing Group-of-10 currency against the dollar so far this month.

item.title: Tokyo Inflation Picks Up, Keeping BOJ on Track for Further Hike
item.description: Tokyo’s key inflation gauge picked up for the first time in eight months, keeping the Bank of Japan on a trajectory to raise interest rates further.

item.title: China’s Hardware Tech Stocks Look to Earnings to Sustain Rally
item.description: Chinese hardware technology stocks have been on a tear. The next challenge is showing the earnings to back it up.

item.title: BHP’s Incoming CEO Reshuffles Leadership Before Official Start
item.description: BHP Group’s incoming Chief Executive Officer Brandon Craig has announced changes to the company’s leadership team before he officially assumes the role on July 1.

item.title: Apple Supplier Lingyi Set for HK Debut After $1.1b Offering
item.description: Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. a Chinese maker of electronic devices, is set to begin trading in Hong Kong on Friday after raising HK$8.3 billion ($1.06 billion) in a share sale, part of the city’s busiest month for listings this year.

item.title: Singapore Grads Battle Low-Paid Traineeships Stigma to Get Hired
item.description: As the class of 2026 join the race to find jobs, unemployed college graduates in Singapore are taking a last-ditch shot at getting ahead: temporary government-funded gigs that earn them half the median first paycheck.

item.title: Indonesia Opens Door to Dirty Money to Fund Prabowo’s Plans
item.description: Indonesia is dangling unprecedented legal protections for investors in President Prabowo Subianto’s sovereign wealth fund, a move analysts warn could attract money with questionable origins and further erode the reputation of Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

item.title: Musk Starts Expanding X Payments to More Users After Delay
item.description: Elon Musk is expanding access to digital payment venture X Money to a subset of premium users on social network X, part of his bid to turn the platform into an “everything app.”

item.title: Wall Street Embraces the Dollar as Warsh’s Fed Activates Bulls
item.description: The dollar is wrapping up one of its best months in a year as a raft of Wall Street banks see a turnaround of fortunes for the US currency.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain as Traders Weigh Hormuz Flows After Ship Attack
item.description: Oil held a gain as traders weighed the outlook for a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz after a cargo ship was attacked in the waterway.

item.title: Asia Stocks Set for Choppy Open on Tech Volatility: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were set for a mixed open Friday after a volatile Wall Street session, where renewed selling in megacap technology shares offset gains fueled by resilient US economic data.

item.title: Trump Administration Asks OpenAI to Stagger Release of AI Model
item.description: The Trump administration has asked OpenAI to stagger the release of an upcoming powerful artificial intelligence model, according to a person familiar with the matter, nearly two weeks after rival Anthropic PBC suspended its most capable offerings from the market under regulatory pressure.

item.title: Permira-Backed Womenswear Retailer Reformation Files for US IPO
item.description: Reformation Inc., a sustainable womenswear brand that’s backed by private equity firm Permira, filed for a US initial public offering, disclosing growing sales.

item.title: South Korea’s ‘Ant’ Army Is Driving an AI Market Frenzy
item.description: Some 14 million retail investors are piling into shares of two big tech giants, but worries are growing about what happens if chip demand dries up.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: ホルムズ海峡で船舶に攻撃、米PCE価格と消費支出、アップル値上げ、JPモルガンCEO後任争いで新局面、自動車部品マレリの資産取得協議

item.title: Trump-Backed Golf Resort Advances as Vietnam Seizes Farmland
item.description: Eviction notices have been sent to farmers in northern Vietnam to make way for a $1.5 billion Trump Organization-branded luxury golf resort project that’s already behind schedule.

item.title: UK’s Crown Estate Profits Fall as Offshore Wind Fees Decline
item.description: Profits at the Crown Estate, the property company that generates income for King Charles III, fell last year as a temporary boost from the licensing of new offshore wind farms began to fade.

item.title: Pentagon Sees Broader Role for AI in Setting Military Targets
item.description: The Pentagon has quietly revised its doctrine on how the US military picks its targets in battle, opening the way for artificial intelligence to make critical wartime decisions in the future.
